quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungary has over 1,500 natural thermal springs, more than any other country in Europe! The tradition of bathing in thermal waters dates back over 2,000 years to Roman times, and Budapest alone has 118 thermal springs that supply the city's famous bathhouses.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals in the Salish Sea
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals in the Strait of Juan de Fuca have fascinating behaviors, from their curious nature to how they care for their pups. Learn about these charming neighbors you might spot from Port Angeles shores.

medium_read.title: The Hidden Language of Flowers: Victorian Messages in Your Garden
medium_read.teaser: Flowers have carried secret meanings for centuries, from roses symbolizing love to lavender representing devotion. Discover the fascinating history of floriography and what messages your garden might be sending.

long_read.title: Inside the Ancient Temperate Rainforests of the Olympic Peninsula
long_read.teaser: The Hoh and Quinault rainforests near Port Angeles are among the rarest ecosystems on Earth, draped in moss and home to trees over 1,000 years old. This beautiful exploration takes you deep into these magical cathedral forests.

port_angeles.title: Bald Eagle Nesting Along the Strait
port_angeles.description: June is an active time for bald eagle families along the Strait of Juan de Fuca. Many eaglets are learning to fly this month, and you can often spot parents teaching their young to fish from vantage points like Ediz Hook and the waterfront trail. The Port Angeles area is home to dozens of nesting pairs!

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart can beat up to ___ times per minute during flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Amazing! Those tiny hearts are incredible powerhouses, beating 1,200 times per minute to fuel their aerial acrobatics!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute — that's 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: A hummingbird's heart actually beats about 1,200 times per minute during flight — that's incredibly fast to power those amazing wings!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the highest metabolism of all animals. Their hearts can beat over 1,200 times per minute while flying, but slow to around 250 beats per minute when resting at night.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is indeed butterfly — such a lovely, delicate-sounding word, just like the creature itself!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound as light and graceful as a butterfly itself? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language, part of the Finno-Ugric family rather than the Indo-European languages spoken by Hungary's neighbors. This makes Hungarian words wonderfully distinctive and often quite poetic.

puzzle_3.question: What type of tree is the largest living organism on Earth?
puzzle_3.correct_response: That's right! Giant Sequoias like 'General Sherman' are the largest living organisms by volume — some weigh over 2 million pounds and can live for 3,000 years!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's actually the Giant Sequoia! The largest, 'General Sherman,' weighs over 2 million pounds and is still growing. While Coast Redwoods grow taller, Sequoias are more massive overall.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The General Sherman tree in California's Sequoia National Park is the largest known living single-stem tree on Earth. It's 275 feet tall, over 36 feet in diameter, and estimated to be between 2,300 and 2,700 years old.
puzzle_3.learn_more_label: Visit General Sherman — National Park Service

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
